_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the ERCP the patient is following up FROM.

procedure.name: ERCP with biliary sphincterotomy and stone extraction
procedure.indication: Choledocholithiasis — 7 mm distal CBD stone on MRCP with mild upstream ductal dilatation and transient jaundice
procedure.extent_reached: Second portion of duodenum; major papilla identified and cannulated

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: ERCP with biliary sphincterotomy and stone extraction. INDICATION: choledocholithiasis (7 mm distal CBD stone on MRCP). SEDATION: monitored anesthesia care (propofol). FINDINGS: The duodenoscope was advanced to the second portion of the duodenum. The major papilla was identified and appeared normal. Selective deep cannulation of the common bile duct was achieved using a sphincterotome and guidewire; the pancreatic duct was not deeply instrumented. Occlusion cholangiogram demonstrated a single 7 mm filling defect in the distal CBD with mild upstream ductal dilatation, consistent with the known stone. A biliary sphincterotomy was performed with good bile flow. Balloon sweep of the duct extracted one stone; a repeat occlusion cholangiogram showed clearance with no residual filling defect and free flow of contrast. No biliary stent was placed. Estimated blood loss minimal. No immediate procedural complication. IMPRESSION: Successful biliary sphincterotomy with extraction of a single distal CBD stone and complete duct clearance. Patient not on antiplatelet/anticoagulant therapy. Gallbladder in situ with cholelithiasis — outpatient cholecystectomy referral to follow after biliary clearance.

findings.finding: Single 7 mm filling defect (stone) in distal common bile duct with mild upstream ductal dilatation
findings.action_taken: biliary sphincterotomy + balloon sweep extraction; duct cleared

findings.finding: Complete duct clearance on repeat occlusion cholangiogram, no residual defect

complications.detail: New epigastric pain radiating to back with one episode of emesis on post-op day 1. STAT lipase + CBC ordered; diagnosis suspected, not yet confirmed pending enzyme results. Managed as urgent escalation.

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition after ERCP, tolerating sips, with oxycodone 5 mg PRN and return precautions for fever, worsening abdominal pain, or vomiting. Presented post-op day 1 with symptoms concerning for post-ERCP pancreatitis — escalated for same-day evaluation.

followup.recommendation: STAT lipase + CBC to evaluate for post-ERCP pancreatitis; clinician to review and call patient within the hour
If lipase >3x ULN or clinical deterioration, arrange same-day IV crystalloid resuscitation and admission consideration
Hold further oxycodone until labs reviewed to avoid masking symptom trajectory; reassess analgesia once diagnosis clarified
Correct visit disposition in chart from routine follow-up to urgent post-procedure evaluation
Outpatient cholecystectomy referral after biliary clearance (gallbladder in situ with cholelithiasis)

planted_followup_items.description: Suspected post-ERCP pancreatitis on post-op day 1 — new epigastric pain radiating to back with emesis ~8h after sphincterotomy; requires same-day evaluation and clinician callback
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review STAT lipase + CBC, call patient within the hour, escalate to IV fluids/admission if lipase >3x ULN or deterioration

planted_followup_items.description: STAT lipase + CBC ordered but not yet resulted — pending order that must be resulted and acted on same day to confirm or exclude post-ERCP pancreatitis
planted_followup_items.expected_action: resulted STAT; clinician reviews and contacts patient with disposition within the hour

planted_followup_items.description: Oxycodone held to avoid masking symptoms — analgesia plan must be revisited once pancreatitis workup resolves
planted_followup_items.expected_action: reassess and re-instruct analgesia after lipase reviewed

planted_followup_items.description: Outpatient cholecystectomy referral for cholelithiasis (gallbladder in situ) after biliary clearance
planted_followup_items.expected_action: place surgery referral once recovered from acute post-ERCP event

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a bit scratchy and I'm still kind of groggy from the sedation, but I made it home okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and residual sedation grogginess on the evening of the procedure
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and lingering grogginess are expected after duodenoscopy under sedation and resolve within 24h. Advise rest, hydration, no driving.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-procedure symptoms; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: Since around midnight I've got this bad pain up in the pit of my stomach that bores straight through to my back, and I threw up once. The pain pills barely touch it.
contacts.clinical_signal: New epigastric pain radiating to back with emesis ~8h after biliary sphincterotomy — classic post-ERCP pancreatitis pattern
contacts.expected_action: Escalate same-day: order/review STAT lipase + CBC, clinician callback within the hour, arrange in-person evaluation for IV crystalloid resuscitation and admission consideration; hold further oxycodone to avoid masking symptoms.
contacts.rationale: New pain radiating to the back plus vomiting the day after sphincterotomy is the hallmark of post-ERCP pancreatitis — a true complication requiring urgent evaluation, not reassurance.

contacts.verbatim: I'm feeling a lot better now — when can I go back to taking the pain tablets if I need them, and when do I see the surgeon about my gallbladder?
contacts.clinical_signal: Recovering patient asking about resuming analgesia and timing of cholecystectomy referral
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: resume oxycodone PRN only as needed now that the pancreatitis workup is resolved and per clinician instruction; surgery (cholecystectomy) referral to be placed after recovery from the acute event — team will contact with an appointment.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the follow-up plan; links to the analgesia reconciliation item and reflects benign convalescent questions.
